webhooks.name: vagentcard.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for AgentCard resources that enforces spec.targetRef is present and prevents multiple AgentCards from targeting the same workload (apiVersion + kind + name) within a namespace, ensuring a one-to-one mapping between AgentCards and their backing workloads.

webhooks.name: mraycluster.ray.openshift.ai
webhooks.purpose: Mutates RayCluster resources on create/update to inject an OAuth proxy sidecar container, TLS secret volumes, mTLS certificate-generation init containers, and TLS-related environment variables into head and worker pod specs. The validating webhook ensures these injected components (OAuth proxy, volumes, init containers, env vars, service account name) remain immutable and blocks enabling native ingress.

webhooks.name: vraycluster.ray.openshift.ai
webhooks.purpose: Mutates RayCluster resources on create/update to inject an OAuth proxy sidecar container, TLS secret volumes, mTLS certificate-generation init containers, and TLS-related environment variables into head and worker pod specs. The validating webhook ensures these injected components (OAuth proxy, volumes, init containers, env vars, service account name) remain immutable and blocks enabling native ingress.

webhooks.name: clusterservingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources on create/update to ensure model format priorities are consistent (no duplicate priorities across runtimes for the same model format and protocol, and same priorities within a single runtime for the same format), and validates multi-node WorkerSpec constraints including GPU resource types, parallel size minimums, blocked environment variables, and disallowed structural changes like removing an existing WorkerSpec.

webhooks.name: inferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.pod-mutator
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that intercepts Pod creation for KServe InferenceService workloads, injecting storage initializer init-containers (with cloud-storage credentials), inference-logging/batching agent sidecars, metrics-aggregator sidecars, GKE GPU node selectors, and optionally modelcar (OCI image) volume mounts so that model-serving pods are fully configured at admission time.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.defaulter
webhooks.purpose: Mutating defaulter for LLMInferenceService resources that applies schema defaults and attaches local-model-cache labels and annotations. It matches the resource's model URI against cluster-scoped LocalModelCache and namespace-scoped LocalModelNamespaceCache entries (namespace-scoped takes precedence), setting the corresponding PVC name and source URI so the controller can mount a pre-cached model volume instead of downloading at runtime.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceService v1alpha1 resources on create and update, enforcing router/gateway cross-field constraints (e.g., HTTPRoute refs vs inline spec exclusivity, managed-gateway compatibility), parallelism rules (pipeline and data parallelism mutual exclusivity, worker requiring parallelism, positive values, data/dataLocal co-requirement), scheduler config validity (replicas > 0, inline-vs-ref exclusivity), scaling and actuator-backend consistency across decode and prefill workloads, and immutability of total parallelism size on updates.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.defaulter
webhooks.purpose: Mutating defaulter for LLMInferenceService resources that applies schema defaults and attaches local-model-cache labels and annotations. It matches the resource's model URI against cluster-scoped LocalModelCache and namespace-scoped LocalModelNamespaceCache entries (namespace-scoped takes precedence), setting the corresponding PVC name and source URI so the controller can mount a pre-cached model volume instead of downloading at runtime.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validating admission webhook for the LLMInferenceService v1alpha2 custom resource that enforces structural and semantic constraints on create and update operations, including router/gateway cross-field consistency, parallelism configuration rules, scheduler config validity, LoRA adapter name uniqueness and safety, autoscaling settings (HPA/KEDA mutual exclusivity, WVA variant cost format, replica bounds), actuator backend consistency between decode and prefill workloads, and immutability of total parallelism size on updates.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update, rejecting configs that use the forbidden baseRefs field or specify invalid scheduler replicas (must be > 0), and emits warnings when well-known configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update, rejecting configs that use the forbidden baseRefs field or specify invalid scheduler replicas (must be > 0), and emits warnings when users modify or delete well-known (built-in) configs.

webhooks.name: servingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources on create/update to ensure model format priorities are consistent (no duplicate priorities across runtimes for the same model format and protocol, and same priorities within a single runtime for the same format), and validates multi-node WorkerSpec constraints including GPU resource types, parallel size minimums, blocked environment variables, and disallowed structural changes like removing an existing WorkerSpec.

webhooks.name: clusterservingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources on create/update by enforcing model format priority consistency (no duplicate priorities across runtimes for the same model format, and consistent priorities within a single runtime), validating multi-node WorkerSpec constraints (pipeline/tensor parallel sizes, GPU resource types, container count limits, disallowed env vars), and checking for blocked environment variables.

webhooks.name: inferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.pod-mutator
webhooks.purpose: Mutates KServe-managed Pods at creation time by injecting storage initializer containers (for model downloading), credential volume mounts and environment variables (for accessing model storage), inference logger/batcher agent sidecars, metrics aggregator configuration, GKE accelerator node selectors, and optional modelcar (OCI image source) support. It reads the InferenceService and cluster configuration to determine which sidecars and credentials each Pod needs.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.defaulter
webhooks.purpose: Mutating defaulter for LLMInferenceService resources that applies schema defaults, reads the inference service ConfigMap to check if local model caching is enabled, and then matches the service's model URI against existing LocalModelCache and LocalModelNamespaceCache resources to set labels and annotations pointing to the correct local model PVC (with namespace-scoped caches taking precedence over cluster-scoped ones).

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for LLMInferenceService (v1alpha1) that rejects invalid create/update requests by enforcing router cross-field constraints (e.g., HTTPRoute refs vs inline spec, managed vs custom gateway compatibility), parallelism rules (worker requires parallelism, pipeline and data parallelism mutual exclusivity, data/dataLocal co-requirement, positive values), scheduler config validity (replicas > 0, inline vs ref mutual exclusivity), workload scaling rules, and immutability of total parallelism size on updates.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.defaulter
webhooks.purpose: Mutating defaulter for LLMInferenceService resources that applies schema defaults, reads the inference service ConfigMap to check if local model caching is enabled, and then matches the service's model URI against existing LocalModelCache and LocalModelNamespaceCache resources to set labels and annotations pointing to the correct local model PVC (with namespace-scoped caches taking precedence over cluster-scoped ones).

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validating admission webhook for LLMInferenceService v1alpha2 resources on create and update. It enforces structural invariants including router/HTTPRoute cross-field constraints, parallelism configuration consistency (pipeline vs data), immutability of parallelism size on updates, scheduler config completeness, LoRA adapter name uniqueness and safety, scaling/replica mutual exclusivity with WVA/HPA/KEDA actuator rules, decode-prefill actuator backend consistency, managed DRA annotation correctness, and confidential model spec validation.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update, ensuring baseRefs are not used (recursive refs disabled), scheduler replicas are positive if specified, and running optional injected validation logic. It also emits warnings when well-known configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update by rejecting configs that use the forbidden baseRefs field (recursive refs are disabled) and ensuring scheduler replicas are greater than zero, while also emitting warnings when well-known configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: servingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources on create/update by enforcing model format priority consistency (no duplicate priorities across runtimes for the same model format, and consistent priorities within a single runtime), validating multi-node WorkerSpec constraints (pipeline/tensor parallel sizes, GPU resource types, container count limits, disallowed env vars), and checking for blocked environment variables.

webhooks.name: notebooks-validation.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates updates to Notebook resources, specifically preventing removal of the MLflow instance annotation while a notebook is running. This guards against a broken state where MLflow environment variables persist in the pod but the associated RoleBinding is deleted immediately, leaving the MLflow client with credentials but no permissions.

webhooks.name: notebooks.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook for Kubeflow Notebook CRs (create/update) that injects operational sidecars and configuration: resolves container images from OpenShift ImageStreams, injects a kube-rbac-proxy authentication sidecar with TLS, mounts trusted CA certificate bundles, mounts pipeline runtime image configs and Elyra/Data Science Pipelines secrets, injects cluster-wide proxy environment variables, sets MLflow tracking environment variables, applies a reconciliation lock on creation, and blocks webhook-only pod-template changes on running notebooks to prevent unintended restarts (marking them as update-pending instead).

webhooks.name: mraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates RayCluster resources on create/update to enforce platform-specific networking defaults. On OpenShift clusters (detected via Route API availability), it sets the secure trusted network annotation to true and forcibly disables enableIngress to enforce Gateway API-only access with centralized authentication; on non-OpenShift clusters, it sets the annotation to false.

webhooks.name: mraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates RayCluster resources on create/update to enforce platform-specific networking defaults. On OpenShift clusters (detected via Route API availability), it sets the secure trusted network annotation to true and forcibly disables enableIngress to enforce Gateway API-only access with centralized authentication; on non-OpenShift clusters, it sets the annotation to false.

webhooks.name: vraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates RayCluster resources on create and update, ensuring the cluster name matches a DNS-compatible lowercase alphanumeric pattern and that all worker group names within the spec are unique.

webhooks.name: vcohort.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates Cohort resources on create and update by checking the spec's fair sharing configuration and resource groups, ensuring they conform to Kueue's rules (e.g., valid resource group definitions and fair sharing settings). Deletes are always allowed without validation.

webhooks.name: mmodelregistry.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating admission webhook for ModelRegistry custom resources. The mutating handler applies default values to the spec on create/update, while the validating handler enforces cluster-wide name uniqueness on create (by listing all existing ModelRegistry resources) and validates the registry spec fields on both create and update.

webhooks.name: vmodelregistry.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating admission webhook for ModelRegistry custom resources. The mutating handler applies default values to the spec on create/update, while the validating handler enforces cluster-wide name uniqueness on create (by listing all existing ModelRegistry resources) and validates the registry spec fields on both create and update.

webhooks.name: servingruntime.modelmesh-webhook-server.default
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources that enforces autoscaling configuration rules on multi-model (ModelMesh) runtimes, including checking that the autoscaler class is supported, target utilization percentage is a valid 1-100 integer, HPA metrics are allowed, min/max replica bounds are consistent, and that static replicas are not set alongside an HPA autoscaler.

webhooks.name: vaitenant.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook that enforces AITenant resources are only created in the configured infrastructure namespace. It rejects any AITenant whose namespace does not match the pre-configured AITenantNamespace, ensuring tenant placement is controlled at admission time.

webhooks.name: vmaasauthpolicy.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates MaaSAuthPolicy resources on creation by ensuring the target namespace contains an existing Tenant CR, preventing auth policies from being created in namespaces that have not been initialized for MaaS tenancy.

webhooks.name: vmaassubscription.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook that rejects creation of MaaSSubscription resources unless a Tenant CR already exists in the same namespace, ensuring proper tenant initialization before subscriptions can be created.

webhooks.name: minferencegraph-v1alpha1.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Provides mutating defaulting (currently a no-op) and validating admission control for KServe InferenceGraph v1alpha1 resources. The validator enforces that each graph node step's serviceURL is a well-formed cluster-internal URL whose target namespace matches the InferenceGraph's own namespace, rejecting cross-namespace references and public hostnames to prevent credential leakage.

webhooks.name: minferenceservice-v1beta1.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates that new InferenceService resources have a name within the allowed length and are not created in the protected applications namespace (determined by the DSCInitialization CR), and provides a mutating defaulter endpoint that is currently a no-op.

webhooks.name: mutating.pod.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates incoming Pods that have RAY_USE_TLS enabled on their kserve-container or worker-container by injecting TLS-related volumes, volume mounts, and an init container that copies Ray TLS certificates from a shared Secret into the pod, enabling mTLS communication between Ray cluster nodes used for distributed model serving.

webhooks.name: validating.isvc.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates that new InferenceService resources have a name within the allowed length and are not created in the protected applications namespace (determined by the DSCInitialization CR), and provides a mutating defaulter endpoint that is currently a no-op.

webhooks.name: validating.nim.account.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates NIM Account creation to enforce a singleton constraint: only one Account resource is allowed per namespace. On create, it lists existing Accounts in the target namespace and rejects the request if one already exists; updates and deletes are always permitted.

webhooks.name: vinferencegraph-v1alpha1.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Provides mutating defaulting (currently a no-op) and validating admission control for KServe InferenceGraph v1alpha1 resources. The validator enforces that each graph node step's serviceURL is a well-formed cluster-internal URL whose target namespace matches the InferenceGraph's own namespace, rejecting cross-namespace references and public hostnames to prevent credential leakage.

webhooks.name: vogxserver.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for OGXServer resources on create and update. It rejects unknown distribution names (checked against an injected registry), duplicate provider IDs, model-resource references to non-existent providers, and adoption annotations whose value equals the CR name (which would cause Deployment name conflicts), and warns on deprecated distribution names.

webhooks.name: mutate-pod.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that intercepts Pod create/update requests in Spark job namespaces, looks up the owning SparkApplication, and defaults the pod spec with settings from the SparkApplication CR—including volumes, env vars, config maps, affinity, tolerations, node selectors, security contexts, GPU resources, sidecar/init containers, ports, owner references, memory limits, and scheduling configuration—differentiating between driver and executor pods.

webhooks.name: mutate-sparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that sets default values on SparkApplication resources when they are created or updated. It only applies defaults when the application is in the 'New' or 'Invalidating' state, using the operator's webhook scheme to populate unset fields with their default values.

webhooks.name: validate-scheduledsparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates ScheduledSparkApplication resources on create and update by ensuring the metadata.name is a valid DNS-1035 label, preventing downstream failures when the operator later creates SparkApplication and Service resources that require DNS-compliant names. The main validate logic is currently a stub (TODO).

webhooks.name: validate-sparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook (CustomDefaulter) for ScheduledSparkApplication resources, intended to set default field values on create and update. The current implementation is a no-op stub that logs the mutation request but does not modify any fields.

webhooks.name: mresourceflavor.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for Kueue ResourceFlavor objects. The mutating webhook ensures a 'ResourceInUse' finalizer is added on creation, while the validating webhook checks that nodeLabels, nodeTaints (unique by key+effect, valid label keys/values), and tolerations conform to Kubernetes conventions on create and update.

webhooks.name: vresourceflavor.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for Kueue ResourceFlavor objects. The mutating webhook ensures a 'ResourceInUse' finalizer is added on creation, while the validating webhook checks that nodeLabels, nodeTaints (unique by key+effect, valid label keys/values), and tolerations conform to Kubernetes conventions on create and update.

webhooks.name: mraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates and validates RayCluster resources for Kueue queue management. The mutating webhook defaults the local queue name, suspends jobs that should be managed by Kueue (checking namespace selectors and ancestor ownership), and sets the managedBy field. The validating webhook ensures Kueue-managed RayClusters do not use in-tree autoscaling, have at most 7 worker groups, do not use the reserved 'head' group name, and have valid topology-aware scheduling annotations.

webhooks.name: vraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates and validates RayCluster resources for Kueue queue management. The mutating webhook defaults the local queue name, suspends jobs that should be managed by Kueue (checking namespace selectors and ancestor ownership), and sets the managedBy field. The validating webhook ensures Kueue-managed RayClusters do not use in-tree autoscaling, have at most 7 worker groups, do not use the reserved 'head' group name, and have valid topology-aware scheduling annotations.

webhooks.name: mclusterqueue.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for Kueue ClusterQueue resources. The mutating webhook adds a ResourceInUse finalizer on creation, while the validating webhook enforces structural correctness of resource groups (no duplicate resources or flavors, valid quota quantities), namespace selector validity, mutual exclusivity of admission check configurations, preemption policy consistency, fair sharing settings, and borrowing/lending limit constraints (e.g., lending limit must not exceed nominal quota, and limits must be nil when no cohort is set).

webhooks.name: vclusterqueue.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for Kueue ClusterQueue resources. The mutating webhook adds a ResourceInUse finalizer on creation, while the validating webhook enforces structural correctness of resource groups (no duplicate resources or flavors, valid quota quantities), namespace selector validity, mutual exclusivity of admission check configurations, preemption policy consistency, fair sharing settings, and borrowing/lending limit constraints (e.g., lending limit must not exceed nominal quota, and limits must be nil when no cohort is set).

webhooks.name: mworkload.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Defaults and validates Kueue Workload resources. The mutating webhook strips minCount from podSets when the PartialAdmission feature gate is disabled, while the validating webhook enforces constraints on podSet definitions (e.g., reserved resource keys), admission assignments, reclaimable pod counts, admission check pod-set updates, and immutability of spec and admission fields once a quota reservation exists.

webhooks.name: vworkload.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Defaults and validates Kueue Workload resources. The mutating webhook strips minCount from podSets when the PartialAdmission feature gate is disabled, while the validating webhook enforces constraints on podSet definitions (e.g., reserved resource keys), admission assignments, reclaimable pod counts, admission check pod-set updates, and immutability of spec and admission fields once a quota reservation exists.

webhooks.name: mrayjob.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for KubeRay RayJob resources managed by Kueue. The mutator assigns a default local queue, suspends jobs so Kueue controls admission, and sets the managedBy field; the validator enforces that Kueue-managed RayJobs shut down after finishing, do not reference existing clusters, disable autoscaling, limit worker groups to seven, forbid the reserved 'head' group name, and checks topology-aware scheduling annotations.

webhooks.name: vrayjob.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for KubeRay RayJob resources managed by Kueue. The mutator assigns a default local queue, suspends jobs so Kueue controls admission, and sets the managedBy field; the validator enforces that Kueue-managed RayJobs shut down after finishing, do not reference existing clusters, disable autoscaling, limit worker groups to seven, forbid the reserved 'head' group name, and checks topology-aware scheduling annotations.

webhooks.name: mmpijob.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Provides mutating and validating admission webhooks for Kubeflow MPIJob resources managed by Kueue. The mutating webhook defaults the local queue name, suspends jobs that should be managed by Kueue (checking ancestor ownership and namespace selectors), and sets the managedBy field; the validating webhook enforces queue-name immutability on updates and validates Topology-Aware Scheduling annotations on each MPI replica spec.

webhooks.name: vmpijob.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Provides mutating and validating admission webhooks for Kubeflow MPIJob resources managed by Kueue. The mutating webhook defaults the local queue name, suspends jobs that should be managed by Kueue (checking ancestor ownership and namespace selectors), and sets the managedBy field; the validating webhook enforces queue-name immutability on updates and validates Topology-Aware Scheduling annotations on each MPI replica spec.

webhooks.name: mjobset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating admission webhook for JobSet resources (jobset.x-k8s.io). The mutating webhook defaults the local queue label, suspends jobs that should be managed by Kueue (walking owner references to avoid double-managing child jobs), and sets the managedBy field to the MultiKueue controller when the cluster queue has a MultiKueue admission check. The validating webhook enforces queue-name label immutability on updates and validates topology-aware scheduling (TAS) pod-set annotations on each replicated job template.

webhooks.name: vjobset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating admission webhook for JobSet resources (jobset.x-k8s.io). The mutating webhook defaults the local queue label, suspends jobs that should be managed by Kueue (walking owner references to avoid double-managing child jobs), and sets the managedBy field to the MultiKueue controller when the cluster queue has a MultiKueue admission check. The validating webhook enforces queue-name label immutability on updates and validates topology-aware scheduling (TAS) pod-set annotations on each replicated job template.

webhooks.name: mstatefulset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Manages Kueue integration for StatefulSet workloads: the mutating webhook propagates queue-name labels, pod-group annotations, and suspension state onto the StatefulSet's pod template so that pods are created under Kueue's control; the validating webhook enforces that the queue name is immutable while replicas are running, the pod spec is immutable while suspended, replicas can only scale to/from zero, and the workload priority class cannot change after creation.

webhooks.name: vstatefulset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Manages Kueue integration for StatefulSet workloads: the mutating webhook propagates queue-name labels, pod-group annotations, and suspension state onto the StatefulSet's pod template so that pods are created under Kueue's control; the validating webhook enforces that the queue name is immutable while replicas are running, the pod spec is immutable while suspended, replicas can only scale to/from zero, and the workload priority class cannot change after creation.

webhooks.name: mleaderworkerset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating admission webhook for LeaderWorkerSet resources integrated with Kueue. The mutating webhook defaults the local queue label, suspends workloads that should be managed by Kueue, and injects priority-class labels and pod-group annotations into leader and worker pod templates. The validating webhook ensures the queue name and priority class are immutable on update, validates queue name format and topology requests on create, and prevents modifications to pod template specs while the workload is suspended.

webhooks.name: vleaderworkerset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating admission webhook for LeaderWorkerSet resources integrated with Kueue. The mutating webhook defaults the local queue label, suspends workloads that should be managed by Kueue, and injects priority-class labels and pod-group annotations into leader and worker pod templates. The validating webhook ensures the queue name and priority class are immutable on update, validates queue name format and topology requests on create, and prevents modifications to pod template specs while the workload is suspended.

webhooks.name: vagentruntime.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for AgentRuntime resources that enforces three invariants on create/update: no two AgentRuntimes in the same namespace may target the same workload (apiVersion+kind+name), mTLS mode must be compatible with the chosen authBridgeMode, and tlsBridgeMode=enabled is only permitted with proxy-sidecar or lite authBridgeModes (since the TLS bridge lives in the Go forward proxy).

webhooks.name: localmodelnamespacecache.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LocalModelNamespaceCache resources on create, update, and delete. On create/update, it ensures all referenced node groups exist; on delete, it prevents removal if the cache is still referenced by any InferenceService or LLMInferenceService.

webhooks.name: localmodelnamespacecache.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LocalModelNamespaceCache resources on create, update, and delete. On create/update, it ensures all referenced node groups exist; on delete, it prevents removal if the cache is still referenced by any InferenceService or LLMInferenceService via label matching.

webhooks.name: vworkspace.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for Workspace resources on create and update. It ensures the referenced WorkspaceKind exists, that the selected imageConfig and podConfig IDs are valid options defined in that WorkspaceKind, and that pod template metadata (labels and annotations) conforms to Kubernetes validation rules.

webhooks.name: vworkspacekind.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating admission webhook for WorkspaceKind resources (kubeflow.org/v1beta1) that enforces spec correctness on create/update (pod metadata, environment variables, imageConfig/podConfig values including redirect-chain cycle detection and port references) and prevents deletion when any Workspace still references the WorkspaceKind. On update it performs incremental validation of only changed config entries and lazily queries Workspace usage counts to block removal of in-use options.

webhooks.name: connection-notebook.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for Kubeflow Notebook resources that validates and injects data-connection secrets. When a Notebook is created or updated with the 'opendatahub.io/connections' annotation, the webhook verifies that each referenced Secret exists and that the requesting user has 'get' permission on it (via SubjectAccessReview), then mutates the Notebook's container spec to add or remove the corresponding envFrom secretRef entries.

webhooks.name: podmonitor-injector.opendatahub.io
webhooks.enable_condition: MonitoringServiceName component enabled OR MonitoringServiceName component enabled
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that automatically injects a monitoring scrape label (monitoring.opendatahub.io/scrape=true) onto PodMonitor and ServiceMonitor resources created or updated in namespaces labeled for ODH monitoring, after verifying that the platform-level Monitoring CR exists (i.e., monitoring is enabled).

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v2-defaulter.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that sets default field values on DataScienceCluster v2 resources during create/update. When ModelRegistry is Managed and RegistriesNamespace is empty, it defaults the namespace; when Kserve is Managed and NIM ManagementState is empty, it defaults NIM to Managed.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v2-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DataScienceCluster v2 admission requests by enforcing a singleton constraint (denying creation if one already exists) and rejecting any DSC where Kueue's managementState is set to Managed, which is no longer supported.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v1-defaulter.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating defaulting webhook for DataScienceCluster v1 resources. When ModelRegistry is Managed and RegistriesNamespace is empty, it sets RegistriesNamespace to the default value; when Kserve is Managed and NIM.ManagementState is empty, it defaults NIM to Managed.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v1-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DataScienceCluster v1 resources on create and update. On create, enforces singleton (only one DSC instance allowed) and rejects Kueue with ManagementState Managed. On update, rejects Kueue Managed state and prevents v1 API modifications when v2-only components are currently Managed, avoiding silent data loss that would occur during v1-to-v2 conversion (v2-only fields default to Removed).

webhooks.name: dscinitialization-v2-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DSCInitialization v2 resources on create and delete. On create, enforces a singleton constraint by denying if another DSCInitialization instance already exists. On delete, prevents removal while any DataScienceCluster resource still exists in the cluster.

webhooks.name: hardwareprofile-notebook-injector.opendatahub.io
webhooks.enable_condition: Kserve component enabled OR Workbenches component enabled
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook that intercepts create/update of Notebooks, InferenceServices, and LLMInferenceServices to inject hardware resource requirements (CPU, GPU, memory identifiers), node scheduling configuration (tolerations, nodeSelector), and Kueue labels from a referenced HardwareProfile CR specified via annotations. It also handles cleanup of previously applied settings when the hardware profile annotation is removed or changed.

webhooks.name: connection-llmisvc.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for LLMInferenceService resources that automatically injects data-connection details (model URI, image pull secrets, service account) into the spec based on a referenced Secret's connection-type annotation. On create or update, it validates the connection annotation, resolves the referenced Secret to build the appropriate URI (plain URI, S3, OCI, or HuggingFace), and patches the object; on connection removal or replacement it cleans up previously injected fields.

webhooks.name: connection-isvc.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for KServe InferenceService resources that automatically injects model-serving connection details (storage URIs, S3 storage keys, OCI image pull secrets, service account names) into the ISVC spec based on a platform connection annotation, and cleans up or replaces those injected fields when the annotation is updated or removed.

webhooks.name: dscinitialization-v1-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Enforces singleton and dependency rules for DSCInitialization v1 resources: on create, denies the request if a DSCInitialization instance already exists (singleton pattern); on delete, denies the request if any DataScienceCluster instance still exists, preventing removal of the initialization resource while dependent clusters are active.

webhooks.name: validator.clustertrainingruntime.trainer.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates ClusterTrainingRuntime resources on create and update by checking that replicated job specs are well-formed, and emits a deprecation warning on create if the runtime is labeled as deprecated per the Kubeflow Trainer deprecation policy.

webhooks.name: validator.trainjob.trainer.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates TrainJob resources on create and update by resolving the referenced runtime (e.g., JobSet) from a registry and delegating to that runtime's ValidateObjects method, rejecting requests that reference an unsupported runtime or fail runtime-specific validation rules.

webhooks.name: validator.trainingruntime.trainer.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates TrainingRuntime resources on create to ensure that replicated jobs marked as ancestors (trainer, model-initializer, dataset-initializer) have exactly 1 replica and contain the required named container for their ancestor type (e.g., 'node' for trainer, 'model-initializer' for model-initializer).

webhooks.name: validator.pytorchjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates PyTorchJob resources on create and update, enforcing DNS-1035 naming, requiring valid replica types (Master/Worker), ensuring the Master has exactly 1 replica, verifying each replica spec contains a container named 'pytorch' with a defined image, and validating elastic policy constraints including deprecation of elasticPolicy.nProcPerNode in favor of nprocPerNode.

webhooks.name: validator.paddlejob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates PaddleJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is DNS-1035 compliant, replica specs contain only valid replica types (Master, Worker), each replica has containers with images defined, and at least one container is named 'paddle'. On updates, it also validates RunPolicy immutability constraints.

webhooks.name: validator.xgboostjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates XGBoostJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is DNS-1035 compliant, a Master replica with exactly 1 replica exists, only Master and Worker replica types are used, every container has an image, and each replica spec includes a container named 'xgboost'.

webhooks.name: validator.jaxjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates JAXJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is a valid DNS-1035 label, that jaxReplicaSpecs are present with only the 'Worker' replica type, that each replica spec has containers with images defined, and that at least one container is named 'jax'.

webhooks.name: validator.tfjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates TFJob resources on create and update, enforcing that the job name is a valid DNS-1035 label, that tfReplicaSpecs are present with at least one container named 'tensorflow' per replica that has an image set, and that at most one Chief or Master replica role is defined.

webhooks.name: conversion.dscinitializations.dscinitialization.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Implements CRD conversion webhook for DSCInitialization, converting between the v1 (spoke) and v2 (hub) API versions. It maps spec fields (ApplicationsNamespace, Monitoring, TrustedCABundle, DevFlags) and status fields between versions so that clients using either API version see a consistent resource.
